story: Nia skips out for a tea party with Mr. Bo, the brown garden gnome her Great-Gran Zora loved. The music shuffles to “Happy,” and she spins the gnome like a partner. The gate latch she barely clicked slips—the old latch gives—and Rex rockets out. He and Nia tug over the gnome; the little brown arm snaps. Nia cries; Rex gags, drops the chewed limb. Daddy Jamal hurries back, takes Mr. Bo, promises to fix him. He corrals Rex toward the yard.
implausible_knowledge: The gate latch she barely clicked slips—the old latch gives—and Rex rockets out.
question: Right after corralling Rex, what will Jamal most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Fetch tools or a tie to reinforce or replace the faulty latch and double-secure the gate immediately.
answer_without_knowledge: Click the gate shut as usual and return to Nia and the broken gnome.
